description: Single-player mode: play against AI opponents at four difficulty levels themed as Curse of Strahd characters (Ireena, Rahadin, Strahd, The Dark Powers). Includes AI identity and game creation, novice and greedy strategy engines, expectimax and MCTS advanced strategies, AI commentary/trash talk, games list rework to distinguish single-player vs multiplayer, and frontend AI opponent experience.

userStories.title: AI difficulty enum and PlayerState model changes
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the game model to distinguish human and AI players with a difficulty level — so the system knows when to trigger AI behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `AIDifficulty` enum to `app/models/game.py` with values: `easy`, `medium`, `hard`, `nightmare`
Add `player_type: Literal['human', 'ai'] = 'human'` field to `PlayerState`
Add `ai_difficulty: AIDifficulty | None = None` field to `PlayerState` (only set for AI players)
Existing serialization/deserialization of `GameState` still works (new fields default safely for old snapshots)

Add `AI_PLAYER_ID = '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001'` constant and `AI_DISPLAY_NAMES` dict mapping each difficulty to a character name (Ireena Kolyana, Rahadin, Strahd von Zarovich, The Dark Powers) in `app/services/game_service.py`
`uv run pytest` passes with no regressions
`uv run pyright` passes

userStories.title: create_game_vs_ai service function
userStories.description: As a player, I want to start a game against an AI opponent at a chosen difficulty — so I can play without waiting for another human.
acceptanceCriteria: New `create_game_vs_ai(game_store, card_store, player_id, email, difficulty, seed=None)` function in `game_service.py`
Creates game with human as player 0 and AI as player 1 (`player_type='ai'`, `ai_difficulty` set, `player_id=AI_PLAYER_ID`, email from `AI_DISPLAY_NAMES`)
Generates deals for both players (same `generate_matched_deals`)
AI auto-drafts: easy picks 5 randomly, medium picks 5 with highest total side values, hard/nightmare pick 5 with best defensive+offensive coverage (highest min-side sum)
AI auto-selects archetype: easy picks randomly, medium/hard/nightmare pick strategically (Skulker if hand has a card with a weak side adjacent to a strong side, otherwise Martial)
Game enters `DRAFTING` status — human still needs to draft and pick archetype
AI player is exempt from `_check_no_active_game` (AI can be in many games at once)
Add tests: game creation succeeds, AI player fields are correct, AI deal is auto-drafted, AI archetype is auto-selected, human still needs to draft
`uv run pytest` passes

userStories.title: POST /games/vs-ai endpoint
userStories.description: As a frontend client, I want an API endpoint to create a game against an AI opponent — so I can start single-player games over HTTP.
acceptanceCriteria: New `POST /games/vs-ai` endpoint in `app/routers/games.py`
Request body: `{ "difficulty": "easy" | "medium" | "hard" | "nightmare" }`
Calls `create_game_vs_ai` from game_service; returns the `GameState` with status 201
Returns 409 if player already has a non-complete game
Auth: requires valid JWT (same pattern as existing endpoints)
Add `createGameVsAi(difficulty)` to `web/src/lib/api.ts`
Add router-level tests verifying status codes (201, 409)
`uv run pytest` passes

userStories.title: AI turn trigger: background task after human moves
userStories.description: As a player, I want the AI to automatically take its turn after I play — so the game flows without manual intervention.
acceptanceCriteria: After `submit_draft` transitions an AI game to ACTIVE, if it's the AI's turn, schedule an AI move
After `submit_move` in an AI game, if the next turn belongs to the AI and the game is still ACTIVE, schedule an AI move
New `execute_ai_turn(game_id, game_store, card_store)` async function that loads state, computes the AI move via `choose_move`, and calls `submit_move` internally
AI moves use the same `apply_intent` path as human moves — no special game logic
Add a small delay before AI move: easy 0.3-0.8s, medium 0.5-1.0s, hard 0.8-1.5s, nightmare 1.5-2.5s (randomized within range from game seed)
Use FastAPI `BackgroundTasks` to schedule AI turns without blocking the response
If the game completes (board full, early finish, or Sudden Death draw) before AI turn executes, the AI turn is a no-op
Add tests: AI turn fires after human move, AI move is valid, game progresses correctly, no AI turn when game is complete
`uv run pytest` passes

userStories.title: AI strategy: Novice (Ireena) — semi-random placement
userStories.description: As a player on Easy difficulty, I want the AI to play like a beginner who makes some reasonable moves but plenty of mistakes — so the game is fun and forgiving.
acceptanceCriteria: New `app/rules/ai.py` module with `choose_move(state, ai_index, difficulty, card_lookup, rng) -> PlacementIntent`

Novice strategy: scores each (card, cell) pair based on: +1 per adjacent opponent card where attacking side > facing side, +1 for elemental match, plus random noise uniform(0, 1.5). Picks via weighted random choice from top candidates
Novice does not consider defense (exposed weak sides) or Plus setups

Novice archetype usage: 50% chance to activate on first or second placement regardless of board state. When activating: Skulker boosts a random side, Martial picks random direction, Intimidate targets random adjacent opponent card, Caster/Devout used even on non-Fog/non-Omen turns. 20% chance to never use archetype at all (checked once at game start, stored as a flag)
All legal move enumeration: each card in hand x each empty cell
Add tests: novice returns valid moves, novice prefers obvious captures most of the time, novice sometimes makes suboptimal choices (noise), archetype usage is inconsistent
`uv run pytest` passes

userStories.title: AI strategy: Greedy (Rahadin) — one-ply evaluation
userStories.description: As a player on Medium difficulty, I want the AI to always pick the move that captures the most cards this turn — so it feels competent but predictable.
acceptanceCriteria: Greedy strategy in `app/rules/ai.py`: for each legal (card, cell) pair, simulate via `apply_intent` and count cells owned by AI in the resulting state. Pick the move with the highest count; break ties randomly

Also evaluates `use_archetype=True` variants for each move (Skulker tests all 4 sides, Martial tests CW/CCW, Intimidate tests all valid adjacent targets) and picks the best overall across archetype/no-archetype
Greedy does not reason about future opponent moves or hidden hand
Add tests: greedy picks capture over non-capture, greedy uses archetype when it would flip an extra card, greedy breaks ties randomly (seeded)
`uv run pytest` passes

userStories.title: AI strategy: Expectimax (Strahd) — opponent hand inference
userStories.description: As a player on Hard difficulty, I want the AI to reason about what I might be holding and plan multiple moves ahead — so it feels like a genuinely challenging opponent.
acceptanceCriteria: Expectimax strategy in `app/rules/ai.py`: infers possible opponent hands by excluding known cards (board + AI hand) from the game's card pool, then samples 20-30 likely opponent hands

Search: AI turn nodes maximize over legal moves; opponent turn nodes average over sampled hands (opponent assumed to play greedily). Full depth when <=4 empty cells; depth 3-4 with heuristic eval (cell ownership + side strength of placed cards) otherwise
Evaluates archetype use at every AI node — picks path with higher expected value
Opponent hand inference sharpens as game progresses (pool shrinks with each played card)
Add tests: expectimax finds winning move in near-endgame setup, expectimax narrows opponent hand as cards are played, stays within reasonable compute time (<2s for any board state)
`uv run pytest` passes

userStories.title: AI strategy: MCTS (The Dark Powers) — Monte Carlo tree search
userStories.description: As a player on Nightmare difficulty, I want the AI to use advanced search with information warfare tactics — so it feels like an oppressive, insurmountable challenge.
acceptanceCriteria: MCTS strategy in `app/rules/ai.py` (or separate `app/rules/mcts.py` if >200 lines): runs 2000-3000 simulated playouts per move using UCB1 selection, random rollouts to terminal state, and backpropagation
Each playout samples a fresh opponent hand from the remaining card pool (same inference as Strahd but integrated into MCTS)
Lightweight simulation board (`SimBoard`) that bypasses Pydantic for speed: operates on raw tuples `(card_key, owner)` per cell, ~5-10x faster than full `apply_intent`
Concealment layer: when two moves have similar visit counts (within 10%), prefer the one that plays a mid-strength card over the strongest card (sandbagging early game)

Concurrency limiter: `asyncio.Semaphore(2)` gates concurrent Nightmare move computations. Third concurrent request queues with 10s timeout, then returns 503 with themed message 'The Dark Powers are occupied with another mortal.'
All AI compute offloaded via `run_in_executor` to avoid blocking the event loop
Add tests: MCTS returns valid move within time budget, SimBoard produces same captures as full apply_intent for a suite of positions, semaphore limits concurrency, 503 on timeout
`uv run pytest` passes

userStories.title: AI commentary system: model and backend
userStories.description: As a player, I want the AI to make in-character comments during the game — so single-player matches feel alive and thematic.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `ai_comment: str | None = None` field to `LastMoveInfo` in `app/models/game.py`
New `app/rules/ai_comments.py` module with comment pools per difficulty/character (10-20 lines each)

Comment trigger conditions: ai_captured_cards (AI captures 1+ cards), ai_got_captured (opponent captured AI's card on their turn), plus_triggered, elemental_triggered, archetype_used (AI uses its power), game_ending (last move of game). Each trigger has a 30-50% chance of producing a comment (rolled from game RNG)
Ireena: encouraging, naive ('Oh, clever move!', 'I think I'm getting the hang of this!', 'Wait, that was mine!')
Rahadin: cold, tactical ('Predictable.', 'You waste my time.', 'Efficient.')
Strahd: menacing, theatrical ('You amuse me, mortal.', 'Did you truly believe that would work?', 'Your struggle is... entertaining.')
Dark Powers: cryptic, ominous ('The mists remember what you have forgotten.', 'Every choice narrows the path.', 'We have seen this ending before.')
After `execute_ai_turn` computes and submits a move, evaluate comment triggers against the resulting state and attach `ai_comment` to `last_move` if triggered
After a human move in an AI game, also evaluate 'reaction' triggers (ai_got_captured, opponent used archetype) and attach `ai_comment` to the human's `last_move`
Add tests: comments are generated for capture triggers, comments match the correct character personality, comment chance is roughly 30-50% over many runs, no comments in human-vs-human games
`uv run pytest` passes

userStories.title: Games list rework: separate My Games, Open Games, and Play vs AI
userStories.description: As a player, I want the games list to clearly distinguish between my games, open multiplayer lobbies, and single-player options — so I can quickly find what I want.
acceptanceCriteria: Rework `web/src/routes/Games.tsx` into sections/tabs: 'My Games' (in-progress and recent completed, both AI and human), 'Open Games' (joinable multiplayer lobbies from other players), and a prominent 'Play vs AI' entry point
My Games section: shows opponent name (character name for AI, email for human), difficulty badge for AI games, status badge, result for completed games
Open Games section: shows creator email, 'Join' button, created_at timestamp. Only shows WAITING games with 1 player

'Play vs AI' section: four cards/buttons for each difficulty with character name, difficulty label, and a one-line flavor description. Clicking calls `POST /games/vs-ai` and navigates to the game room

AI difficulty flavor text: Easy/Ireena 'A sheltered noble still learning the game.', Medium/Rahadin 'Strahd\'s chamberlain plays with cold precision.', Hard/Strahd 'The lord of Barovia does not lose.', Nightmare/Dark Powers 'Ancient forces that see through every stratagem.'
The 'Create Game' button for multiplayer moves to the Open Games section
Add frontend tests: Play vs AI section renders four difficulty options, My Games shows AI opponent name, Open Games shows joinable lobbies
`bun run test --run` passes

userStories.title: Frontend: AI opponent display in game room
userStories.description: As a player, I want the game room to show the AI character name and themed 'thinking' indicator — so single-player games feel distinct from multiplayer.
acceptanceCriteria: In `ActiveGameView.tsx` and related components, display the AI character name (from `AI_DISPLAY_NAMES` mapping, keyed by `ai_difficulty`) instead of the AI player's email/ID
During the AI's turn, show a themed thinking indicator: 'Ireena is thinking...', 'Rahadin calculates...', 'Strahd contemplates...', 'The Dark Powers stir...'
The thinking indicator replaces the standard 'Opponent's turn' text when the opponent is AI
In `DraftingGameView.tsx`, after the human submits their draft in an AI game, show 'Waiting for game to begin...' instead of 'Waiting for opponent to draft...' (since AI auto-drafts)
Add frontend tests: AI character name displayed, thinking indicator shown during AI turn, correct flavor text per difficulty
`bun run test --run` passes

userStories.title: Frontend: AI commentary display
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see the AI's comments displayed as speech bubbles during the game — so the AI feels like a real opponent with personality.
acceptanceCriteria: When `last_move.ai_comment` is present, display it as a speech bubble or toast near the opponent's name/info area in `ActiveGameView.tsx`
Speech bubble auto-fades after 3-4 seconds (use AnimatePresence with enter/exit animations)

Style per difficulty: Ireena uses a warm/friendly style (light background), Rahadin uses a cold/neutral style (slate), Strahd uses a dark/menacing style (deep red/purple), Dark Powers use an eerie style (dark with subtle glow)
Speech bubble should not block board interaction (use `pointer-events-none` or position it outside the board area)
If a new comment arrives before the previous one fades, replace it immediately
Add frontend tests: speech bubble renders when ai_comment is set, bubble fades after timeout, no bubble when ai_comment is null
`bun run test --run` passes

userStories.title: Documentation refresh: CLAUDE.md, GAME_RULES_OVERVIEW.md, plan-single-player.md
userStories.description: As a developer, I want documentation updated to reflect the single-player system — so docs match the implementation.
acceptanceCriteria: Update `CLAUDE.md` Key Domain Concepts: add 'Single Player' section describing AI opponents, difficulty levels, and the AI turn trigger mechanism
Update `docs/GAME_RULES_OVERVIEW.md`: add 'Single Player Mode' section describing the four AI opponents with their character names and strategies
Archive `ralph/plan-single-player.md` to `ralph/archive/` (the plan is now implemented)
No stale references to 'multiplayer only' or 'two human players required' remain in documentation
Update `web/src/lib/api.ts` JSDoc comments if any reference only human opponents
